agent_maintenance: Treat this file as the product brief for AI work on this repo. When a feature or convention becomes stable and recurring, extend rules_summary, ui_guide, or http_api here (keep it accurate; avoid bloating).
rules_summary: Default read-only. No silent mutations. Cassandra nodetool panels and all kubectl-sidecar commands must stay read-only (informational nodetool subcommands only; no `compact`, `repair`, `decommission`, etc.).
Prefer simple, neat, fast, and elegant changes: minimal diffs, reuse helpers, avoid breaking existing flows; run Python `unittest discover -s tests` after backend edits when tests exist.
Write/destructive kubectl or API actions require explicit UI confirmation and server acknowledged confirmed flag.
kubectl exec uses -c kafka for broker shell; each exec sets export JMX_PORT=<random high port> to avoid JMX bind collisions.
sanitize_name pattern ^[A-Za-z0-9_.-]+$ for topic/group/namespace and connector user input.
AWS profile dropdown uses a fixed list in backend/aws_profiles.py (not ~/.aws/credentials); session key p → AWS_PROFILE on every kubectl.
AWS (cloud=aws): subprocess_env merges ~/.aws/credentials keys for the selected profile into each kubectl/aws child process.
Home page keeps last listed clusters + selected entry in sessionStorage and restores when cloud/region/profile match.
Top nav workload pages are hidden until session verify; once visible they are disabled (gray, non-clickable) when stack replicas are 0.

Elasticsearch/OpenSearch pages: allocation JSON body is inside Allocation explain panel; Cat shards supports sorting by index+shard and has local index-pattern field synced with indices toolbar. Index-name and shard substring fields filter already-loaded tables on input (no re-fetch); health/state still apply on Refresh (server).
Kafka Consumer groups (list): Topics size (MB) sums on-broker topic log sizes (du under /bitnami/kafka/data) for topics assigned to each group; default row order is largest first.
Header k8s-strip shows tenant_id from PostgreSQL clarisite_management.users (single-row hint) when session is verified and the query succeeds.
All panel/log outputs support wrap toggle checkbox.
kubectl log streaming: GET /api/k8s/logs/stream?workload=&pod=&container= (session); leaving a page calls stopWorkloadLogStream().
Clingine log streaming uses the same endpoint but workload=clingine runs kubectl exec tail -f /root/clingine/log/servers.root.log (not kubectl logs).
Bump VERSION in server.py and mirrored cache-bust / footer on every release.
kubectl context must remain visible in the header.

navigation_and_homepage: Home is always available at #/ and is the landing page.
Before session verification succeeds, hide every workload page in the top nav and show only Home.
After verification succeeds, show all known workload pages in the top nav.
If a workload exists in routing but stack probe says replicas=0, keep its nav item visible but disabled (gray, non-clickable, aria-disabled). Do not hide it after verification.
Top-nav state must be derived from cached session + cached stack probe in one pass to avoid flicker or temporary enable/disable races.
When a user lands on a blocked workload route, keep the current hash and render an in-page callout instead of redirecting to Home.
Disabled nav items must not navigate when clicked.
layout_conventions: A page may start with a short muted operational note, then workload Pod status, then subject sections and/or standalone panels.
Subject sections use titled separators (for example: Brokers, Topics, Groups) and each subject owns its own panel grid or custom layout.
Unless a page explicitly defines subjects, a DB page may remain a flat page composed of panels in reading order.
Default panel grids use responsive cards; panels can be normal width, full-width, or page-specific custom spans.
Supported panel placement semantics for future changes: full page width; 2/3 width aligned left or right; 1/3 width aligned left, center, or right.
When a page needs non-default proportions, keep them explicit in code/comments rather than relying on accidental CSS behavior.
table_and_output_conventions: Tables with size/count/lag/bytes/disk-like columns must sort numerically, never alphabetically.
If a table represents largest/heaviest items, default the initial row order to descending size or descending magnitude.
Scrollable table wrappers and sortable headers are the standard presentation for dense operational tables.
Keep outputs readable: prefer structured tables for tabular data and preformatted output for raw command/HTTP results.
Panel outputs support wrap toggles; exec panels also support JSON view when implemented.
Avoid long explanatory prose inside data panels when concise status + table(s) communicate the issue better.

home.purpose: Connect and verify cluster context before any workload page becomes available.
sections: One connect panel with cloud, region, AWS profile, list clusters, cluster pick/manual cluster name, optional namespace, Connect & verify, Clear session.
behavior: Restore last successful connection defaults when cloud/region/profile match.
AWS profile dropdown comes from the backend preset list, while credentials are loaded from ~/.aws/credentials only into child processes.
Home owns the connect flow; other pages must not try to replace it.

groups: Custom asymmetric layout: left ~1/3 Consumer groups list (client substring filter on loaded table), right ~2/3 state/members panels, full-width lag row below
partition_leadership_balance: Rendered as driver cards, not a single monolithic summary
Drivers currently include Largest topics spread, Partition count spread, RF=1 topics

important_rules: Kafka Pod status and Kafka replica counting must only include real broker StatefulSet pods matching glassbox-kafka-<n>; exclude lookalikes such as kafka-exporter.
Partition leadership balance must stay generic for any broker count (1, 2, 3, ...).
Largest topics spread should be judged cluster-wide and size-weighted, not only by within-replica delta.
Leadership-balance tables should present largest or most important rows first and stay sortable.
Connector-specific panels require a connector name and should guide the user when missing.
Panel substring filters apply to the last loaded response on input (no re-fetch); Refresh reloads from the cluster.

clickhouse.structure: Flat page: parameters panel, diagnostics/data panels grid (Tables in database has client substring filter on loaded table), custom SQL panel, logs.
important_rules: Parameters toolbar owns pod, time window, event table, tenant UUID, absolute time range, and database explorer inputs.
Panels are read-first diagnostics; custom SQL exists separately and requires confirmation for writes.

indices: Cat indices and recovery panels with shared filters/index pattern
shards: Cat shards, shard stores, shard-level health, named-index shard views
stats: Cluster/nodes stats, pending tasks, thread pool, ILM status
others: Templates, aliases, metadata slice, nodes info

important_rules: Allocation explain JSON body belongs inside its own panel.
Indices toolbar and shard toolbar index-pattern inputs stay synchronized.
Custom path is GET-only and conservatively guarded.

postgresql.structure: Flat page: Pod, Catalog, Tables (information_schema as sortable table + client substring filter), Custom SQL, Logs.
important_rules: Catalog and table exploration are fixed read-only queries.
Custom SQL keeps a last-10 successful history per browser tab.

clingine.structure: Pod dropdown, Pod status, Keyspaces, Describe keyspace, Tables (with clickable table names), Custom CQL, Nodetool grid (KEYSPACE/TABLE fields for tablestats), application log stream.
important_rules: Each pod is standalone (embedded Cassandra + local volumes /root/data/recent0 and /root/data/recent1); there is no shared cluster across pods.

CQL runs as cqlsh on glassbox-cassandra-0 (GB_STS_CLINGINE_CQLSH_PROXY_POD) with CQLSH_HOST=clingine-external-<n> and CQLSH_PORT=8186 (GB_STS_CLINGINE_EXTERNAL_PREFIX, GB_STS_CLINGINE_CQL_PORT); Clingine pods have no cqlsh.

Clingine nodetool is best-effort via clingine_nodetool_readonly: each invocation is wrapped in a 5-attempt bash loop with 1s sleep (for i in 1..5; do nodetool.sh -h localhost -p 30081 <op> && break || sleep 1; done). JMX defaults to -p 30081 (the actual listening port; not 7199); GB_STS_CLINGINE_JMX_PORT=digits pins a port; off/none/-/0 omits -p (with -p 30081 as a final fallback on flaky stderr).
GET /api/clingine/nodetool op=tablestats requires nt_keyspace and nt_table (single CF).
Logs panel streams servers.root.log via kubectl exec tail -f, not container stdout.

cassandra.structure: Flat page: Pod, Keyspaces (with client substring filter on loaded table), Describe keyspace, Tables in keyspace, Nodetool read-only panels (status, tablestats, tpstats, gcstats, compactionstats, proxyhistograms), Custom CQL, Logs.
important_rules: Keyspaces panel is the entry point and can feed the keyspace inputs below.
Custom CQL keeps a last-10 successful history per browser tab.
Nodetool runs in-pod via GB_STS_NODETOOL or /opt/bitnami/cassandra/bin/nodetool -h localhost; GET /api/cassandra/nodetool with allowlisted op only.

routes.response: { ok, profiles[], default_profile, meta{ source: builtin } } — fixed list, does not read ~/.aws

routes.notes: version, name, kafka_pod and pod prefixes for kafka-connect/clickhouse/es/os
Home defaults from data/sensitive/last_connected.json + regions + clouds
current_context, cluster_server, cloud, aws_profile
verified + session payload + workload_log_containers map

routes.body: { connect?, cluster_name?, namespace?, aws_profile?, cloud?, region? }
routes.effect: Optional aws eks update-kubeconfig / az aks get-credentials; then cluster-info; writes data/sensitive/last_connected.json

routes.notes: Pod replica counts per known Glassbox component in session namespace
Batched pod status: get pods JSON + top pod + top node + events (filtered); used by workload pages
topics_min / topics_substring apply to panel topics_partition_counts only
kubectl exec kafka pod: du -sk /bitnami/kafka/data/*; response table is per-topic total MB (sorted desc) + default_num_brokers (read-only)
409 + needs_confirmation when risky and confirmed false
kubectl exec + curl to 127.0.0.1:8083 in kafka-connect pod; UI filters loaded table/stdout on input (panel_filter optional, unused by default)
Password from data/sensitive/.ch_password; explorer panels return table JSON; default DB via GB_STS_CLICKHOUSE_DATABASE (glassbox)
409 when SQL not read-only and confirmed false

kubectl exec + curl to 127.0.0.1:9200 in ES pod; idx_substring/shards_substring query params accepted but cat indices / shard tables use full rows server-side — UI filters by substring on the loaded table
Custom GET path only; 409 if path not on allowlist
Same filtering behavior as Elasticsearch panel (UI substring filters on loaded tables)
Custom GET path only; 409 if path not on allowlist
SSE text/event-stream; workload-specific pod/container allowlist; clingine uses exec tail -f on CLINGINE_LOG_FILE
Fixed SELECT via psql; tables panel includes table JSON column; requires .pg_password; psql path via GB_STS_PSQL if not on PATH
SELECT tenant_id FROM clarisite_management.users LIMIT 1; pod from query or stack probe; ok with tenant_id null when unavailable
409 when SQL not read-only and confirmed false
Fixed read-only CQL via cqlsh; panel=keyspaces returns table (Keyspace name column) plus stdout
kubectl exec bash -lc nodetool -h localhost <op>; read-only allowlist; GB_STS_NODETOOL for binary path
409 when CQL not read-only and confirmed false
CQL via clingine_cqlsh_via_cassandra_proxy: cqlsh on Cassandra pod, CQLSH_HOST/CQLSH_PORT to clingine-external-<n>:8186; panel=tables returns table JSON

clingine_nodetool_readonly: best-effort with built-in 5x retry/1s sleep; JMX defaults to -p 30081; GB_STS_CLINGINE_JMX_PORT=digits pins port, off/none/-/0 omits -p (final fallback retries -p 30081 on flaky stderr)
409 when CQL not read-only and confirmed false

postgresql.psql_notes: Server resolves psql: GB_STS_PSQL if set, else Bitnami/common paths, else PATH

clingine.cqlsh_on_proxy: GB_STS_CQLSH path on Cassandra pod if needed
clingine.nodetool_jmx_notes: Default: -p 30081 (the listening port; not 7199). Each call is wrapped in a 5-attempt bash retry loop with 1s sleep to absorb intermittent NoSuchObjectException / Failed to connect. GB_STS_CLINGINE_JMX_PORT=digits pins port; off|none|-|0 omits -p first then falls back to -p 30081 on flaky JMX stderr.

cassandra.cqlsh_notes: Server resolves cqlsh: GB_STS_CQLSH if set, else /opt/bitnami/cassandra/bin/cqlsh and common paths, else PATH
cassandra.cqlsh_host_notes: cqlsh host: GB_STS_CASSANDRA_CQL_HOST else CASSANDRA_HOST / POD_IP / CASSANDRA_BROADCAST_ADDRESS / 127.0.0.1; port from GB_STS_CASSANDRA_CQL_PORT or CASSANDRA_CQL_PORT_NUMBER or 9042
